environmentDefault: Backend: Node.js + Express; DB: SQLite (better-sqlite3); Test runner: Jest + supertest; OS: local dev

Auth API registers and returns token.testTitle: Register new user with valid payload returns 201 and JWT
Auth API registers and returns token.preConditions: API running; users table empty for test email
Auth API registers and returns token.expectedResult: HTTP 201; body contains user (email matches) and non-empty token
Auth API registers and returns token.postConditions: User row exists; can authenticate with same credentials

Auth API logs in and returns token.testTitle: Login with registered credentials returns 200 and JWT
Auth API logs in and returns token.preConditions: User jane@example.com registered with known password
Auth API logs in and returns token.testSteps: 1. Register user. 2. POST /api/v1/auth/login with email and password

Posts API creates and fetches a post.testTitle: Authenticated user creates post and public GET returns it
Posts API creates and fetches a post.testSteps: 1. POST /api/v1/posts with Bearer token. 2. GET /api/v1/posts/:id without token
Posts API creates and fetches a post.testData: title=Hello MVC, content=This is my first post in the API service.
Posts API creates and fetches a post.expectedResult: Create returns 201 with post id; GET returns 200 and same title
Posts API creates and fetches a post.postConditions: Post persisted; visible via read API

Posts API rejects creating a post withou.testTitle: Create post without Authorization header is rejected
Posts API rejects creating a post withou.testSteps: 1. POST /api/v1/posts with title and content only (no Bearer token)
Posts API rejects creating a post withou.testData: title=No Auth, content=This request should be rejected by auth middleware.
Posts API rejects creating a post withou.postConditions: No new post from unauthenticated request
